meta.name: Teriyaki Chicken
meta.subtitle: Glossy soy-ginger glaze with a weeknight-fast cook time

source.name: SSA canonical cuisine recipes
notes: Designed for Asian Fusion overlay planning.
Uses amount{value,unit} across all ingredients for schema compliance.
Steps use id+title+instructions[] (rich form), with timers using minutes fields.

summary: Tender, pan-seared chicken coated in a quick teriyaki glaze—sweet-salty, ginger-garlic forward, and perfect over rice with crisp vegetables.

yield.notes: Serves 4 with rice and a vegetable side. Increase chicken to 2 lb for hearty servings.

required.label: Large skillet or sauté pan
required.notes: Use a wide pan to reduce steaming and build a glossy glaze.

required.label: Small saucepan (optional)
required.notes: You can reduce the sauce directly in the skillet; saucepan is optional for tighter control.

notes: Use tamari for gluten-free (verify certified GF).
Swap sesame oil and garnish if sesame-sensitive.

pairings.notes: Add quick pickles or a crunchy veg for contrast to the sweet-salty glaze.

refrigerator.notes: Store chicken and rice separately when possible for best texture.

freezer.notes: Freeze cooked chicken with sauce; thaw overnight and reheat gently with a splash of water.

reheat.notes: Reheat gently to avoid drying. Add a teaspoon or two of water to loosen glaze.

handling: Avoid cross-contamination when prepping raw chicken.
Cook chicken to a safe internal temperature (165°F / 74°C) unless your household standard differs.
safety.notes: If using thighs, they can be cooked a bit higher for tenderness (170–180°F / 77–82°C).

leftoversRemix: Chop leftovers for teriyaki fried rice.
Use as a wrap filling with shredded cabbage and a squeeze of lime.

ingredients.label: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.notes: About 1.5 lb. Breast works too—reduce cook time to avoid drying.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: About 1/3 cup. Use tamari for gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Water
ingredients.notes: Helps the sauce simmer and reduce without burning early.

ingredients.label: Honey
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp; optional but adds shine and rounded sweetness.

ingredients.label: Rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: About 1 Tbsp. Substitute apple cider vinegar in a pinch.

ingredients.label: Toasted sesame oil
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp; add at the end for aroma.

ingredients.label: Cornstarch
ingredients.notes: About 1 Tbsp. Mix into a slurry with a little of the sauce or water.

ingredients.label: Scallions (green onions), sliced
ingredients.notes: About 2–3 scallions. Use part white/part green.

ingredients.label: Sesame seeds
ingredients.notes: About 2 tsp; toast briefly for extra aroma.

ingredients.label: Cooked rice, to serve
ingredients.notes: About 4 cups cooked. Substitute quinoa or cauliflower rice.

ingredients.label: Lime wedges, to serve
ingredients.notes: A squeeze brightens the glaze and balances sweetness.

steps.title: Mix the teriyaki base
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, water, brown sugar, honey (if using), and rice vinegar.
Stir in grated ginger and minced garlic.
In a small cup, mix cornstarch with 1–2 Tbsp of the sauce mixture to form a smooth slurry; set aside.
checks: Sauce tastes sweet-salty and slightly sharp; adjust sugar or vinegar to preference.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
instructions: Pat chicken dry; season lightly with black pepper.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add neutral oil.
Add chicken in a single layer (work in batches if needed). Sear until browned on the first side, 4–5 minutes.
Flip and cook 3–4 minutes more until mostly cooked through.

temperature.notes: Target a lively sizzle; reduce heat if garlic in pan starts to burn later.

checks: Chicken has good browning (fond) on the pan.
If chicken is very thick, extend cook time 1–2 minutes.

steps.title: Add sauce and reduce to a glossy glaze
instructions: Reduce heat to medium. Pour in the teriyaki sauce base (not the slurry yet). Scrape up browned bits from the pan.
Stir the slurry again, then drizzle it into the pan while stirring. Simmer until the sauce thickens and coats the chicken, 2–4 minutes.
Turn off heat and stir in toasted sesame oil.
checks: Sauce is glossy and coats the back of a spoon.
If sauce gets too thick, add 1–2 Tbsp water and stir to loosen.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Slice chicken if desired, or serve whole pieces over rice.
Spoon extra glaze over the top. Garnish with scallions and sesame seeds (optional).
Serve with lime wedges (optional) and a crisp vegetable side.
checks: Taste and adjust: add a squeeze of lime or a splash of vinegar if it feels too sweet.
Add chili crisp or red pepper flakes if you want heat.

notes: Shortcut: Use bottled teriyaki sauce, then add fresh ginger/garlic and reduce to thicken.
For extra shine and cling, keep the simmer gentle after adding slurry to avoid breaking the glaze.
If using chicken breast, pull earlier and glaze briefly to prevent dryness.

adapt.notes: Verify tamari is certified gluten-free. Avoid some imitation soy sauces.

add.notes: Stir in at the end or serve on the side.

adapt.notes: You can also add 1–2 tsp sriracha to the sauce base.

add.notes: Slice thin; add after chicken sears.

adapt.notes: Cook vegetables before thickening the sauce; then glaze everything together.
